_comment: Delivery OS — the standing deploy lane (OS v6 #7). RATIFIED ONCE per repo via the decision-ratification skill, then NEVER hand-edited casually: it is the founder's standing pre-authorization for the deployment-operator agent. Anything not declared here is OUT OF SCOPE and fails closed. The agent reads this file through templates/tools/deploy-lane.mjs; it does NOT widen it. Copy this file to the repo root as `.deploy-lane.json`, fill the targets, ratify, and add the matching .claude/settings.json allow-rules (docs/deploy-lane-setup.md) ONCE.

ratified._comment: Provenance of the standing authorization. Set by the founder at ratification (decision-ratification skill). The agent treats an unratified lane (by="") as OUT OF SCOPE for everything.

actions._comment: Each key is a named action CLASS the founder pre-authorized. `cmd`+`args` are the exact underlying command run for an in-scope invocation; extra args passed on the CLI are APPENDED. `forward_only:true` marks a migration class the operator may run unattended; a down/rollback must NOT be declared here (it is irreversible ⇒ out of scope by design). Remove a class to revoke that authorization.

vercel-deploy-prod.description: Production deploy of the API/SPA on the Vercel+Supabase plane.

supabase-migrate-forward.description: Apply pending forward-only migrations to the system-of-record DB (via the session pooler, per the deploy-vercel-supabase playbook).

guards._comment: Belt-and-braces fail-closed denylist. Even if a class is somehow declared, an underlying command matching any of these substrings is REFUSED. Irreversible/destructive ops are never in any lane.
